mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term profit downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 12-month trailing CPI inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real yields rise sufficiently to attract capital into equities seeking yield, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread condition triggers positive returns in less than 45% of instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate does not exceed random chance (50%) at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a broad demand shock that forces immediate equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside increasing housing starts during a post-recession recovery phase signals synchronized supply-side expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive MoM changes in both INDPRO and HOUST during recovery phases fail to yield positive 20-day returns in at least 50% of cases, or the mechanism shows negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.
